<!-- Thanks for contributing to awesome-agent-reliability!
     Entries are judged against docs/ARCHITECTURE.md (§3 grammar, §4 tags, §5 inclusion
     criteria). CI enforces the mechanical rules; a maintainer applies the merit bar.
     A PR can pass CI and still be declined on curation policy — with a one-line reason. -->

## What does this PR add or change?

<!-- One or two sentences. For a new entry, say which section and why it earns its place. -->

## Entry self-check (for PRs that add or edit entries)

- [ ] Entry follows the one-line schema: `` - [name](url) - honest one-line description. `tag`. ``
- [ ] URL is canonical, first-party, and loads without login.
- [ ] Exactly one tag — `maintained` (runnable, active), `research` (paper/dataset/spec), or `commercial` (closed, paid) — correct per the rubric in [`docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`](../docs/ARCHITECTURE.md).
- [ ] Not a listicle, aggregator, or marketing page.
- [ ] Not redundant with an existing entry — or I explain the distinct capability below.
- [ ] Description is honest, ≤ ~140 chars, present tense, and names any real limitation.
- [ ] Placed in the correct section (and cross-listed with a disambiguation if it legitimately spans two — see ADR-003).

## Distinct capability (only if similar entries exist)

<!-- What does this add that the closest existing entry doesn't? -->
